subject: A man under attack by a flock of ravens, hands clutching his face as the birds claw and peck at him
description: A bearded man in dark clothing recoils in pain, both hands raised to his face as a swarm of ravens descends on him. The birds' wings beat in tight, overlapping silhouettes around his head and shoulders, with sharp beaks and talons visible against his exposed skin. The composition is tight and chaotic — feathers, claws, and the man's anguished posture fill nearly the whole frame. The palette is dominated by inky blacks of the raven plumage, a cold grey-blue background, and the pale flesh tones of the man's hands and forehead. Lighting is low and diffuse, casting the scene in a grim, twilight register that emphasizes the helplessness of the victim and the predatory mass of birds.
foreground: man cowering with hands over face, surrounded by attacking ravens
background: dim grey-blue indeterminate space, possibly an interior or overcast sky
emotion: anguish, fear, recoil — hands shielding face from beaks and claws
